channel.title: NYT > World > Americas
channel.copyright: Copyright 2025 The New York Times Company

item.title: Son of Jair Bolsonaro Says He Will Seek Political Asylum in the U.S.
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro, a son of Brazil’s former president, said he planned to stay in the United States, claiming persecution back home.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova
item.media:description: Eduardo Bolsonaro during a visit to the White House in 2019, during President Trump’s first term.

item.title: Canada Announces Arctic Radar Deal With Australia Amid Trump Threats
item.description: As President Trump talks about U.S. dominance of the Arctic, Canada says it will spend billions to defend the region, including buying new radar technology from Australia.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:credit: Alex Welsh for The New York Times
item.media:description: The northern lights illuminating the sky above a former Distant Early Warning radar station in the Canadian Arctic, a legacy of the Cold War.

item.title: Canadian Who Was in an ‘American Pie’ Video Says ICE Held Her for 12 Days
item.description: Jasmine Mooney, 35, said she was put “in chains” after immigration enforcement officers flagged her visa application paperwork. The former actress was finally allowed to return to Vancouver.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

item.title: Americans Describe Being Detained as Hostages in a Venezuelan Prison
item.description: They were American tourists hoping for a good time, they said. Then they became captives of an autocratic government.
item.dc:creator: Julie Turkewitz
item.media:credit: Caitlin O'Hara for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gregory David Werber, who had been detained in Venezuela, in Tempe, Ariz., on Thursday.

item.title: A Lifeboat to London for Darwin’s Frogs
item.description: Males of the South American species incubate their young in their vocal sacs. The London Zoo recently established a breeding colony to save the frog from extinction.
item.dc:creator: Franz Lidz
item.media:credit: Zoological Society of London, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Adult Darwin’s frogs are about an inch long and weigh less than a couple of paper clips. They are believed to live as long as 15 years in the wild, but they are at risk from a fungal disease.

item.title: Small Plane Crashes Off Roatán Island in Honduras, Killing 12
item.description: The police said five people had survived the crash off Roatán, an island tourist destination. Among the dead was Aurelio Martínez, a popular Honduran musician and politician.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe and Mike Ives
item.media:credit: Honduran Air Force, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A photograph supplied by the Honduran Air Force showing emergency workers caring for an injured person after the crash off Roatán.

item.title: Timeline of Trump’s Deportation Flights, From Alien Enemies Act to Judge’s Order
item.description: A New York Times review of flight data showed that at the time of a federal judge’s order, two flights were in the air, and one had not yet taken off.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Albert Sun and Annie Correal
item.media:credit: Secretaría De Prensa De La Presidencia
item.media:description: A photograph released by El Salvador’s government showed U.S. deportees arriving at the Terrorism Confinement Center in Tecoluca, El Salvador.

item.title: Nayib Bukele, El Salvador’s Leader, Sees Opportunity in Trump’s Deportations
item.description: President Nayib Bukele’s role in the Trump administration’s deportation strategy signals a new level of power and global visibility for the young leader.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal
item.media:credit: Office of the President of El Salvador
item.media:description: The government of El Salvador released photos on Sunday that it said showed deportees from the United States inside the Terrorism Confinement Center in Tecoluca, El Salvador.

item.title: Canada Turns to European Allies Amid Trump Threats
item.description: Days after becoming prime minister, Mark Carney visited Paris and London to highlight deep European friendships as his country fights back against President Trump’s tariffs and annexation talk.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada, left, meeting on Monday with Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain at 10 Downing Street.

item.title: Alone at Sea for 95 Days, a Peruvian Fisherman Clung to Hope
item.description: Máximo Napa Castro, 61, was spotted by the crew of an Ecuadorean tuna boat after he set out from the Peruvian port of Marcona in December.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson
item.media:description: Peruvian fisherman Maximo Napa spent 95 days lost in the Pacific Ocean before being rescued by an Ecuadorian fishing patrol.

item.title: Trump Lawyer Refuses to Answer Judge’s Questions in Deportation Flight Hearing
item.description: The Trump administration tried to have the hearing canceled and sought to remove the judge overseeing it, as White House officials took a confrontational stance.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.media:description: Judge James E. Boasberg directed a Justice Department lawyer to inform him by noon on Tuesday exactly what time the government believes his order stopping the deportation flights went into effect.

item.title: In the Land of the Jaguars
item.description: Brazil’s Pantanal, the world’s largest wetlands, is home to a sizable population of jaguars. Near one tiny outpost, the normally shy animals are remarkably easy to observe.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ham

item.title: Venezuelan Families Fear for Relatives as Trump Celebrates Deportations to El Salvador
item.description: The news that hundreds of migrants were headed to an El Salvador prison caused panic for some Venezuelans, who worried that their loved ones might be among them.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal
item.media:description: The government of Nicolás Maduro, Venezuela’s president, has denounced the United States’ deportation of 238 Venezuelans accused of gang membership to El Salvador.

item.title: How Trump’s Crackdown Is Drastically Driving Down Migration
item.description: Building on Biden-era policies, President Trump is strong-arming regional leaders, deploying military force and shredding decades of precedents when it comes to the U.S.-Mexico border.
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero and Paulina Villegas
item.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
item.media:description: A U.S. Army soldier near the border before a visit by Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth and the White House’s border czar, Tom Homan, in Sunland Park, N.M., last month.

item.title: What Is Tren de Aragua?
item.description: A gang with roots in a Venezuelan prison, the criminal group was at the center of President Trump’s order invoking the Alien Enemies Act of 1798.
item.dc:creator: Eve Sampson
item.media:credit: Christian Monterrosa for The New York Times
item.media:description: Firearms recovered from an operation against Tren de Aragua were on display during a news conference by the Queens district attorney in January.

item.title: Roy L. Prosterman, 89, Dies; Worked to Secure Land for the Rural Poor
item.description: Seeing land rights as the key to lifting up the impoverished, he pushed authoritarian governments as well as emerging democratic ones to distribute farmland.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter

item.title: A Hectic Week in Canadian Politics and Trade, With More Action on the Way
item.description: As Justin Trudeau’s nearly decade-long tenure as prime minister came to an end, new trade threats and tariffs came from Washington.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:credit: Adrian Wyld/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney at Rideau Hall after his swearing-in on Friday.

item.title: In Mexico, a Grisly Discovery of Piles of Shoes, Ovens and Human Remains
item.description: The authorities are investigating the discovery of cremation ovens, human remains, piles of shoes and other personal effects at an abandoned ranch outside Guadalajara.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas
item.media:description: An abandoned ranch outside of Guadalajara in western Mexico, where cremation ovens and human remains were found by a local volunteer group last week.

item.title: G7 Foreign Ministers Agree on Ukraine Policy Amid Tensions Over Trump Tariffs
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio found himself among allies in a fraught moment after President Trump’s tariff policies.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff and Michael Crowley
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio speaking with reporters after the G7 foreign ministers meeting on Friday in La Malbaie, Quebec.

item.title: Mark Carney Becomes Canada’s Prime Minister at Crucial Moment
item.description: Mark Carney, a central banker turned politician, steps into his role at a crucial moment as President Trump sets his sights on Canada’s sovereignty and keeps threatening more tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:credit: Justin Tang/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Mary Simon, the governor general of Canada who represents King Charles III as official head of state, with Mark Carney after he was sworn in on Friday as prime minister.

item.title: Trump Tariffs Leave No Country Room for Exemptions, U.S. Tells Canada
item.description: In talks aimed at finding common ground on tariffs, Canadian officials were told April 2 will be a crucial day in setting the Trump tariff doctrine, and any relief could come later.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:description: Canadian Finance Minister Dominic LeBlanc, center, Industry Minister Francois-Philippe Champagne, and Canada’s ambassador to the United States, Kirsten Hillman, met on Thursday with top Trump administration officials to discuss trade and tariffs.

item.title: Challenged by Trump, Trudeau Rallies Canada as He Leaves Office
item.description: After nearly a decade in power, Justin Trudeau steps down on Friday as prime minister amid a trade war and President Trump’s stated desire to annex Canada.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:credit: Cole Burston for The New York Times
item.media:description: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau speaking to fellow Liberal Party members on Sunday. Mr. Trudeau has spent his last weeks in power challenging President Trump on tariffs and sovereignty.

item.title: How Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum Won Trump’s Praise
item.description: A scientist and leftist with limited foreign policy experience, Claudia Sheinbaum seems to have connected with President Trump with her calm demeanor and toughness on the border.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Kitroeff
item.media:credit: Luis Antonio Rojas for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Claudia Sheinbaum of Mexico in the National Palace last month.

item.title: Canada Gets a New Leader Amid Trump’s Trade War
item.description: Mark Carney, Canada’s incoming prime minister, is no stranger to crises. Now he faces what is arguably the challenge of a lifetime: President Trump. Matina Stevis-Gridneff, the Canada bureau chief for The New York Times, describes what to know about Mr. Carney.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff, Nikolay Nikolov, Rebecca Suner and Gabriel Blanco

item.title: Video: Blood Moon Total Lunar Eclipse
item.description: Earth’s shadow will block most of the light that illuminates the lunar surface, creating what is known as a blood moon.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Miller

item.title: 6 Spectacular North American Train Trips
item.description: The trains are destinations in themselves, offering a mix of spectacular sightseeing, onboard dining and glorious open-air observation cars.
item.dc:creator: Michael Harmon

item.title: Rubio Says a Cease-Fire in Ukraine Could Happen in ‘Days’ if Russia Agrees
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said top diplomats from the Group of 7 allies meeting in Canada should focus on ending the war. And he shrugged off President Trump’s threats to annex Canada.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.media:description: “Here’s what we’d like the world to look like in a few days,” Secretary of State Marco Rubio said in Ireland on Wednesday. “Neither side is shooting at each other — not rockets, not missiles, not bullets, nothing, not artillery.”

item.title: Videos Show Narwhals Using Their Tusks to Play With Their Food
item.description: Researchers observed a number of surprising behaviors by the ivory-sporting whales during an expedition with drones in the Canadian High Arctic.
item.dc:creator: Kaleigh Rogers

item.title: New Trends in Cruising: Exclusive Beach Clubs
item.description: Following the success of its Bahamian island, CocoCay, Royal Caribbean is opening an exclusive beach club a stone’s throw from Nassau, with promises of new opportunities for local tourism. Some islanders are alarmed.
item.dc:creator: Ceylan Yeğinsu and Erin Schaff
item.media:description: CocoCay, the Royal Caribbean cruise line’s private island in the Bahamas.

item.title: An Unexpected Trump Bump for the World’s Centrists
item.description: As President Trump’s “shock and awe” policies radiate around the world, they are galvanizing support for moderate leaders and unifying Europe.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain, left, has reaffirmed Britain’s steadfast support for President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine. Amid a flurry of diplomacy, his poll ratings have surged.

item.title: Trump’s Tariffs on Steel and Aluminum Take Effect
item.description: President Trump imposed a 25 percent tariff on metal imports, sparking new global trade spats as he attempts to shield the U.S. economy from foreign competition.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Jeanna Smialek
item.media:description: An aluminum smelter in Bécancour, Quebec. Canada is by far the largest supplier of both steel and aluminum to the United States.
